curation_notes: Pre-assigned as command-injection from CWE-94, but the patch fixes unsanitized user-controlled config arrays being passed to Yii2's component creation mechanism, enabling injection of event handlers ('on X' keys) and behaviors ('as X' keys) — reclassified to insecure-deserialization. The advisory references three commits: c0a37e15cc (defense-in-depth beforeAction reordering), 7359d18d46 (core RCE fix adding Component::cleanseConfig()), and a270b928f3 (follow-up applying cleanseConfig to a second parameter). The patch commit 7359d18d46 is the primary fix that introduces cleanseConfig() to strip 'on X' and 'as X' keys from user-supplied config arrays, preventing Yii2 event handler/behavior injection. Only 2 non-test source files were changed (ConditionsController.php and Component.php). No sibling controllers share the same vulnerability pattern.
